html:
ADDA is not a mechanical press company. It is a Taiwan OTC micro-cap cooling fan and thermal-module supplier. That correction matters because the stock is being priced off a stale, low-growth “computer peripheral fan” label while the public evidence now points to a genuine revenue inflection in ICT, networking, automotive, energy, and AI-adjacent thermal applications.

The cleanest bullish fact is not the story, it is the monthly revenue tape. ADDA’s official site shows Q1 2026 revenue of TWD 706.4 million, up 30.7% year over year. April then accelerated to TWD 262.3 million, up 31.35% year over year and 6.3% sequentially, bringing January-April revenue to TWD 968.7 million, up 30.9%. Goodinfo flags April as a 44-month high monthly revenue and the January-April cumulative figure as the second-highest in company history and a 15-year high for the period. That is a different setup than the 2025 trough, when full-year revenue fell 5.8% and EPS was only TWD 0.57.

The variant perception is that ADDA screens terribly on trailing earnings. At about TWD 29.30, the stock looks like a 50x trailing P/E small fan maker. That is the wrong lens if the 2026 revenue base is real. Q1 gross margin held at 30.1%, operating margin improved to 9.7%, and the company’s own annual report emphasizes high-performance thermal modules, AI equipment, cloud/server, 5G, automotive electronics, energy storage, and high-static-pressure fan development. This is not proof of a hyperscaler design win, and I would not underwrite one without disclosure. The better thesis is simpler: a small manufacturer with long-standing thermal know-how is seeing its highest revenue run-rate in years just as thermal intensity per device is rising across AI PCs, servers, networking gear, EV electronics, chargers, and industrial power systems.

The key accounting nuance is that Q1 EPS of TWD 0.15 understates the operating inflection. On TWD 706 million of revenue, ADDA generated roughly TWD 69 million of operating profit, but only TWD 16 million of parent-level net income. If below-operating items, financing, FX, tax, and minority leakage remain this punitive, the stock is not cheap. But if revenue sustains near the April run-rate and parent net margin normalizes even to 5.5-7.0%, 2026 EPS can move to roughly TWD 1.6-2.2. If operating margin expands toward 11-12% on TWD 3.0-3.2 billion of revenue, EPS closer to TWD 2.3-2.6 is achievable. That puts the stock at roughly 11-18x forward earnings, not 50x, for a company growing revenue around 30% with a cash-heavy balance sheet.

The balance sheet gives time, but it should not be the whole thesis. Goodinfo shows a Q1 cash balance around TWD 1.57 billion and cash equal to about 39% of assets, while total liabilities are still around half of assets. So this is not a lazy “net cash means free stock” argument. The cash matters because it reduces funding risk, supports the dividend, and gives management room to invest in Vietnam capacity, automation, tooling, and customer qualification. But the upside comes from converting higher revenue into operating profit and parent-level EPS, not from cash hoarding.

Retail context is mixed. This is still a roughly TWD 3.2 billion market-cap stock with limited institutional attention, but it is not undiscovered. Margin financing has risen sharply year to date, and the stock has already moved from the low-20s to around TWD 29-30. That raises volatility risk. It does not kill the thesis because the stock is still below its April 2026 high of TWD 35.50 and the revenue base has moved faster than the share price.

What would make the thesis work: May-July revenue staying above TWD 240-250 million per month, gross margin remaining around 30%, Q2 operating margin confirming Q1, and parent net margin recovering above 5%. If those happen, the market will stop valuing ADDA on 2025 EPS. The cleaner catalyst is not a press release about AI. It is a second quarter where the income statement finally shows that April’s revenue strength is real earnings power.

What would break it: monthly revenue falling back below TWD 210 million, gross margin dropping below 28%, or another quarter where operating profit is healthy but parent EPS remains stuck near TWD 0.15. That would imply either the mix is less valuable than hoped or the corporate structure leaks too much profit to shareholders. Larger competitors can also pressure pricing, and ADDA lacks the scale of Taiwan’s better-known thermal leaders.

At TWD 29.30 today, my targets are:

Bear: TWD 20, down 32%. Revenue fades back toward TWD 2.4-2.5 billion, EPS stays near TWD 0.7-0.8, and the stock trades near book plus a modest cash premium.

Base: TWD 42, up 43%. 2026 revenue reaches about TWD 3.1 billion, operating margin holds near 11%, EPS lands around TWD 2.1, and the stock gets a 20x multiple for a confirmed thermal recovery.

Bull: TWD 68, up 132%. Revenue pushes toward TWD 3.6-3.8 billion as ICT, AI-adjacent, automotive, and energy applications scale, EPS reaches TWD 3.0-3.2, and the market rerates ADDA as a small but real thermal-management growth supplier at 21-22x earnings.
